summary: Implement a conversion plugin to bake noise textures

description.text:
### Overview
Implements a conversion plugin that allows NoiseTexture2D resources to be converted to WebP or PNG files and reimported as CompressedTexture2D.

The motivating use case for this is using a high-resolution noise texture that does not need to be altered after being initially configured - e.g. driving a wave effect in a shader. NoiseTexture2Ds are regenerated when the scene is loaded, and can take many seconds at high resolutions, which can be really annoying when trying to iterate quickly during development, or adversely impact load times in finished projects.

In my own use cases I rarely need to modify a noise texture after setting it up, and if that is required it's still simple enough to recreate it. I assume this is true of many users.

This is my first PR so please excuse me if I missed something obvious!

### Implementation
The existing conversion plugin design does not allow interactivity - conversions are performed immediately and cannot prompt the user for input. This PR also adds the necessary plumbing for asynchronous conversions. Plugins can optionally implement the `convert_async` function which calls a callback when the conversion is complete.

The new `NoiseTextureConversionPlugin` implements this. It opens a dialog to allow the user to select the save location of the new file.

### Alternative implementations
I considered 3 possible implementations of async conversions:
* Add a new `EditorResourceAsyncConversionPlugin` in addition to the existing `EditorResourceConversionPlugin`. Because the interface is different it would require doubling up all the places conversion plugins are currently plumbed in (the filesystem dock, the resource picker, the `find_resource_conversion_plugin` functions etc) with an async version of each.
* Modify the existing `EditorResourceConversionPlugin` to always support asynchronous conversions. `EditorResourceConversionPlugin::convert` would always take a callback, and the existing non-async plugins could just call it immediately.
* Add `convert_async` to the existing `EditorResourceConversionPlugin`, which may return null when the plugin doesn't support async conversions. Likewise, plugins could return null for `convert` if they don't support normal conversions. This way users can try an async conversion if a normal conversion fails.

I opted for the third option because it involved the smallest change to existing code. Option one would involve a lot of almost-duplicate code which didn't seem very elegant. Option two is probably the cleanest but would be an API break and involve changing all existing conversion plugins - I'm a first-time contributor so I don't know what the appetite is for this kind of change

I am more than happy to try one of the other proposed implementations (or indeed anything else) if that would be more desirable.

summary: ColorPicker: Add an intensity slider to all modes for HDR

description.text: - Close https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/1031 Close #99366 This PR adopts a relatively simple approach, without storing extra intensity field in Color class. Adds an intensity slider and limit the max value of RGB to 1 in ColorPicker raw mode. Intensity is inferred from raw RGB and may change after switch color mode. The final color is calculated as `color * pow(2, intensity)`. Also keep the hex LineEdit always visiable and switch it to code if RGB exceeds the valid range. Edit: Depends on #102240 to allow greater values. Edit: Now the intensity slider is added to all modes and should work. I modified the ColorMode and ColorPickerShape so that they only handle colors which the intensity has not been applied.

summary: Material Inspector Panel Scrolls Down Erratically

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.3 .Net
Godot 4.4 .Net

### System information

Win10 22H2 x64
RTX 3070, Driver 572.60

### Issue description
Clicking any setting in the Material Inspector Panel, then clicking anything else in the Material Inspector Panel causes the panel to scroll down by a large amount. This becomes more annoying if the Inspector Panel is made smaller.

Issue occurs in Normal Mode, in Self Contained Mode, and even after setting layout to default.
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/io/data_paths.html#self-contained-mode

Issue does NOT occur if the Inspector Panel is Floating.

### Steps to reproduce

1) Add any node that supports a material, then add a material to the node. For example, add CSGBox with a StandardMaterial3D.
2) Click on the StandardMaterial to view its properties in the Inspector Panel.
3) Open up any section in the Material Inspector Panel. For example, I will open up Transparency.
4) Click on any dropdown within Transparency. For example, Cull Mode's dropdown. Close the dropdown by selecting a dropdown option, or clicking outside the dropdown.
5) Click again anywhere in the Material Inspector Panel that is NOT a setting. For example, click on the actual word "Cull Mode". This will trigger the bug -> The inspector scrolls down by a large amount.
6) This may not seem like a big deal, but let's say you want to change Cull Mode's dropdown, and then the Albedo settings.

Perform the above with a floating Inspector Panel, and the bug will not occur.

summary: The shadow_enabled property on Light3D turns off baked shadowmasks as well

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4-dev7 and later
- Not reproducible in 4.4-dev6 and earlier as those don't have shadowmasks

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Arch Linux #1 ZEN SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 27 Feb 2025 18:09:27 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 570 Series (RADV POLARIS10) - AMD Ryzen 5 2600 Six-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

Currently, turning off the `shadow_enabled` property turns off the shadowmask as well. This goes against the property's documentation, which says that it only affects *real-time* shadows.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/201d04d4-c241-465e-a9a7-41d1fdb51196)

Turning off real-time shadows while keeping shadowmasks on is useful for optimization purposes. Currently, the two ways to achieve a similar effect would be:

- Setting the shadow resolution to lowest and the max distance to 0.1 (0.0 sets it to the camera's far plane). This doesn't give as much performance benefits as it can, since the real-time shadow texture still needs to be drawn
- Setting the light baking mode to static. This approach (in its current implementation) disables specular lighting, which can affect the scene's look quite a lot (example from my game No Downforce):

Static light baking:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3948f92a-161b-4cc9-819c-7077a0dfda8f)

Dynamic light baking with shadowmasks and real-time shadows "disabled" with the first method:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/11b3151c-bb4d-401c-9bb4-599b68f435be)


### Steps to reproduce

1. Open MRP
2. Disable shadows on DirectionalLight3D
3. The shadowmask will be gone as well

summary: 2D Instance Shader Parameter not respected by TileMapLayer

description.text:
### Tested versions

- v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]
- I believe this is the first official release with 2D instance uniform support, so not applicable to older versions

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7700 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.12033.1030) - AMD Ryzen 5 7600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

TileMapLayer seems to erroneously ignore any "instance shader parameters" set on it. If the instance uniform is changed to a normal uniform, then the shader parameter is respected correctly by the TileMapLayer.

As seen in the screenshot below, the material with a normal shader parameter successfully changes both the ColorRect and the TileMapLayer to green, but the material with an instance uniform can only change the ColorRect's color to red; the TileMapLayer's using the instance uniform is not red, and is instead black (the default for a color uniform).

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/347f4d15-787f-4a63-a9c0-175bbb6b15d0)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a shader with the following code:
```
shader_type canvas_item;

instance uniform vec4 color: source_color;

void fragment() {
	COLOR = color;
}
```
2. Create a TileMapLayer, create a tileset with one tile, and add that tile to the layer.
3. Set the material of the TileMapLayer to a new shader material with the shader from step 1.
4. Update the "Color" instance shader parameter on the TileMapLayer and observe that the color does not change.

**Expected Result:** Color of TileMapLayer is changed when instance shader parameter is updated
**Actual Result:** Color of TileMapLayer stays the default color (black) regardless of instance shader parameter

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

The MRP below contains a project that recreates the screenshot and reproduction steps above. It shows that ColorRect respects instance and normal uniforms, while TileMapLayer only respects normal ones.

[2DInstanceShaderParameterWithTileMapLayer.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19062303/2DInstanceShaderParameterWithTileMapLayer.zip)

summary: LineEdit: Fix `caret_force_displayed`

description.text:
After #87674 `caret_force_displayed` would only take effect when LineEdit is being edited, but not focused, which means never 🤷‍♂️
This PR makes it really force the caret, and as a bonus it won't draw inside editor like it used to (it only causes unnecessary redraws).

summary: Godot freezes when modifying Theme Style after recording with OBS (Vulkan crash)

description.text:
### Tested versions

### **Platform and Version**  
- **Godot version:** 4.3 stable  
- **Operating System:** Windows 10  
- **Tested GPUs:** RTX 2060 and GTX 1650  
- **OBS versions:** 31.0.1 and 30.2.3

### **Additional Information**  
- The issue occurs in **all OBS capture modes**, but it is more severe in **"Game Capture"** mode.  

### System information

Godot version: 4.3 stable   - Operating System: Windows 10   - Tested GPUs: RTX 2060 and GTX 1650 (Forward+)

### Issue description

### **Godot freezes when modifying Theme Style after recording with OBS (Vulkan crash)**

**Issue Description**  
When using OBS to record the Godot debugger in **"Game Capture"**, **"Window Capture"**, or **"Screen Capture"** mode, and then modifying the **Style** of a theme in Godot, the engine completely freezes and displays the following errors:  

ERROR: Condition "err != VK_SUCCESS" is true. Returning: ERR_CANT_CREATE at: swap_chain_resize (drivers/vulkan/rendering_device_driver_vulkan.cpp:2718)

ERROR: Condition "err != VK_SUCCESS && err != VK_SUBOPTIMAL_KHR" is true. Returning: FAILED at: command_queue_execute_and_present (drivers/vulkan/rendering_device_driver_vulkan.cpp:2344)


The crash is more severe when using **"Game Capture"**, but it also occurs in other capture modes.  

### **Expected Behavior**  
Godot should not crash when modifying the **Style** of a theme after using OBS.  


### Steps to reproduce

### **Steps to Reproduce**  
1. Open **Godot 4.3 stable** on **Windows 10**.  
2. Run the game in debug mode.  
3. Start recording in OBS using **"Game Capture"**, **"Window Capture"**, or **"Screen Capture"**.  
4. Close the debug session.  
5. Modify the **Style** of a theme (e.g., change a button's border).  
6. Godot freezes and displays the mentioned errors.

- Not reproducible in: **4.3.dev6**

Not reproducible in web builds at https://editor.godotengine.org/, regardless the version.

### System information

Godot v4.3.beta1 - Android - Vulkan (Mobile) - integrated Adreno (TM) 740 -  (8 Threads)

### Issue description

Right clicking is not working as expected while using touchpad (official Galaxy Tab S9 book cover with keyboard and touchpad). It seems it behaves simply as using a left button.

Only workaround is to do a long-press, that would trigger right-click action.

### Steps to reproduce

Create an empty project, have some files in FileSystem dock and some nodes in Scene dock. When trying to right click on a tablet's hardware touchpad (either two finger tap or right click) for example at a file in FileSystem dock, it does nothing. When trying to click same way at a node in Scene dock, it triggers a rename function instead (same way as clicking with a left button).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

New and empty project is sufficient.

summary: Button does not handle pressed=false event

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8]


### System information

Mac OS 15.3.1 Godot 4.3 stable

### Issue description


I have a 2D scene in a canvas layer over top of a 3D scene. 

1. Clicking the container itself will eat (handle) the click events properly.
2. Clicking on a Button will eat the pressed=true event
3. HOWEVER the Button will not eat the pressed=false event.

This is a button who's action_mode is `release`

So basically from my 3D scenes _unhandled_input I can see when I release the button. 

 _unhandledInput(event:): Event mouseButtonEvent InputEventMouseButton: button_index=1, mods=none, pressed=false, canceled=false, position=((1011, 994)), button_mask=1, double_click=false

I never see the pressed=true event, just the pressed=false event. 

Expected behavior: 
- I would expect the `pressed=true` and the `pressed=false` events to be handled by the button.


### Steps to reproduce

Create a 3D camera with a canvas layer that contains that has a standard button with default settings
